name: Google Play Store Publishing
description: Build release APK/AAB and publish to Google Play Console.
when_to_use: When releasing Android app to production or internal testing tracks.
trade_offs: Review times vary (hours to days); must comply with Play Store policies.
implementation_notes: Generate signed AAB (not APK). Configure Play App Signing. Use internal → closed → open → production tracks.
keywords: play store publish release aab signing console

name: Apple App Store Publishing
description: Archive IPA and submit to App Store Connect via Xcode or CI.
when_to_use: When releasing iOS app to TestFlight or App Store.
trade_offs: Apple review is stricter (1-3 days); rejection requires resubmission.
implementation_notes: Archive with Release scheme. Upload via Xcode or xcrun altool. Use TestFlight for beta.
keywords: app store publish archive testflight xcode connect

name: Fastlane CI/CD
description: Automate build; test; screenshot; and deployment for both platforms.
when_to_use: When team needs repeatable release process without manual Xcode/Gradle steps.
trade_offs: Ruby dependency; learning curve for Fastfile DSL; platform-specific lanes.
implementation_notes: Android: fastlane supply for Play Store. iOS: fastlane deliver for App Store. Use match for iOS signing.
keywords: fastlane ci cd automate build deploy supply deliver

name: GitHub Actions for Mobile
description: Cloud-based CI/CD with macOS runners for iOS and Linux/macOS for Android.
when_to_use: When using GitHub for source control and want integrated CI/CD.
trade_offs: macOS runners are expensive (10x Linux); cache Gradle/CocoaPods aggressively.
implementation_notes: Android: ubuntu-latest + setup-java + Gradle cache. iOS: macos-latest + xcodebuild. Use caching actions.
keywords: github actions ci cd mobile android ios runner

name: Code Signing & Provisioning
description: Manage certificates; provisioning profiles; and keystore for release builds.
when_to_use: Always for release builds; critical for CI/CD pipeline automation.
trade_offs: iOS signing is notoriously complex; use match or Xcode Cloud for simplification.
implementation_notes: Android: upload keystore + Play App Signing. iOS: fastlane match or manual cert management. Never commit keystores.

name: Semantic Versioning & Build Numbers
when_to_use: Always; to ensure unique versions for store submission and crash reporting.
trade_offs: Auto-incrementing build numbers in CI require careful state management.
implementation_notes: Android: versionCode (integer; always increment) + versionName (semver). iOS: CFBundleShortVersionString + CFBundleVersion.
keywords: version code name semver build number bundle

name: Build Flavors / Schemes
description: Create variants for different environments (dev; staging; prod) or white-label apps.
when_to_use: When app needs different API endpoints; feature flags; or branding per environment.
trade_offs: Build matrix grows quickly; test all flavors before release.
implementation_notes: Android: build flavors + buildConfigField. iOS: Xcode schemes + xcconfig files. Both: separate bundle IDs.
keywords: flavor scheme variant buildconfig xcconfig environment

name: Beta Distribution (TestFlight / Internal Testing)
description: Distribute pre-release builds to testers before public release.
when_to_use: When QA team or stakeholders need to test before production rollout.
trade_offs: TestFlight requires Apple review for external testers; internal is instant.
implementation_notes: Android: Play Console internal testing track (instant). iOS: TestFlight internal (instant) / external (review).
keywords: beta distribution testflight internal testing track
references: Google Play Internal Testing; Apple TestFlight

name: Crash Reporting & Monitoring
description: Capture and aggregate crash reports with stack traces and device context.
when_to_use: Always for production apps to monitor stability and crash-free rate.
trade_offs: Must upload debug symbols (dSYMs / mapping.txt) for readable stack traces.
implementation_notes: Firebase Crashlytics (both platforms). Sentry. Upload proguard mapping.txt (Android) and dSYMs (iOS) in CI.
keywords: crash reporting crashlytics sentry dsym mapping monitor

name: Feature Flags & Remote Config
description: Toggle features remotely without app update for gradual rollout.
when_to_use: When rolling out risky features; A/B testing; or kill-switch capability.
trade_offs: Increases code complexity; stale flags become tech debt.
implementation_notes: Firebase Remote Config. LaunchDarkly. Custom: fetch config JSON from CDN. Cache with fallback defaults.
keywords: feature flag remote config toggle rollout ab test

name: App Size Monitoring
description: Track APK/IPA size changes across builds to prevent bloat.
when_to_use: When app size exceeds 50MB or is growing rapidly between releases.
trade_offs: Must set thresholds; alert on regressions. Debug vs release size differs significantly.
implementation_notes: Android: APK Analyzer + bundletool size report. iOS: App Thinning Size Report. Automate in CI with size diff check.
keywords: app size apk ipa bundle analyzer thinning monitor
references: Android APK Analyzer; Apple App Thinning

name: Dependency Security Scanning
description: Scan third-party dependencies for known vulnerabilities.
when_to_use: Always as part of CI pipeline; required for enterprise and regulated apps.
trade_offs: False positives require manual triage; some CVEs may not affect your usage.
implementation_notes: Dependabot (GitHub). Snyk. OWASP Dependency-Check. Renovate for automated updates.
keywords: dependency security scan cve vulnerability supply chain

name: AGP 9.x / Gradle 9.x Upgrade
description: Migration rules for bleeding-edge Android build tools (Meerkat+).
when_to_use: When upgrading project to AGP 9.1+ and Gradle 9.3+.
trade_offs: Built-in Kotlin feature makes org.jetbrains.kotlin.android redundant.
implementation_notes: Remove kotlin-android plugin (Built-in Kotlin). Use android.disallowKotlinSourceSets=false for KSP.
keywords: agp 9 gradle 9 meerkat built-in kotlin ksp

name: JDK Toolchain Management
description: Use Gradle Java Toolchain for consistent compile and runtime environments.
when_to_use: Always; to decouple build from runner's JDK version.
trade_offs: Older AGP version used kotlinOptions; AGP 9.x uses top-level kotlin block.
implementation_notes: Use kotlin { jvmToolchain(17) } outside android block. Avoid kotlinOptions.jvmTarget.
keywords: jdk toolchain jvm target java version toolchain
